title: Hide project task dates in Gantt chart
excerpt: When using a Gantt chart view, is there a way to hide timeline column next to each task?  Since it is represented in the chart with milestones or timelines and dates, the dates next to task appears redundant.

posts.author: max.shikhman (Max Shikhman)
posts.cookedHtml: When using a Gantt chart view, is there a way to hide timeline column next to each task? Since it is represented in the chart with milestones or timelines and dates, the dates next to task appears redundant.

posts.author: FrancisElliott (Francis Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Max,

There’s no option to do that specifically as far as I’m aware.

You can close that whole left panel if that helps and then just identify the bars by their labels. Would that work for you?

Screenshot 2023-06-04 at 06.06.091024×352 30.4 KB

posts.author: max.shikhman (Max Shikhman)
posts.cookedHtml: How do I add labels to the bars? I can only see dates. Or can I have both label (group) and date?

posts.author: FrancisElliott (Francis Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml:
Open the settings panel and navigate to the “Label by” section. There are lots of options. If you need something specific, with a bit of work, you can also use a formula column to concatenate, initialize, shorten etc.

Screenshot 2023-06-05 at 15.36.581234×1034 50.4 KB
